name: Wickle
concept: A stout little candle-stub spirit who has burned so low that the wax it shed has pooled into a wide skirt of feet it can never tip over from, and it carries one calm amber flame that has never once flickered.
imagePrompt:
A small painterly creature: a squat, hand-dipped candle-stub spirit, one single connected body, standing upright, full body, facing the viewer, centered.

SILHOUETTE (must read as one clean bold shape): a fat rounded pillar of wax, about one-and-a-half heads tall, wider at the bottom than the top, sitting on a broad heavy skirt of pooled wax that spreads out like a soft scalloped puddle and doubles as its feet — a stable, unfallable base with rounded lobed edges. Two short thick arms are simply heavy wax drips that hang down the sides and end in blunt rounded mitten hands, thick and chunky, fully attached, no fingers. On top of the head, growing directly out of the wax as a solid attached stem, is a short stubby wick nub, and rising from it, fused to it, a single large teardrop flame — a smooth solid leaf-shaped flame with a clean unbroken outline, no sparks, no wisps, no smoke, no floating parts. The flame is a chunky sculpted mass, not thin, at least as wide as the creature's head. Whole outer contour is definite, closed, and readable at tiny size; nothing thinner than a thumb.

PROPORTIONS: chubby, low-slung, soft weight, heavy-bottomed. Big head merged into the body with no neck. Everything rounded, nothing sharp.

PALETTE: body in warm ivory and creamy oatmeal wax with faint honey and soft peach shadows; the one bold colour is the flame — rich glowing amber-apricot, brightening to warm buttery gold at its heart, with a small clean band of soft blue at the very base where it meets the wick. Warm amber lamplight spills down the top of the head and shoulders, so the wax there glows translucent and honeyed, cooling to soft dove-grey shadow at the base.

MATERIAL: painterly oil-on-board. Visible loaded brushstrokes and buttery impasto ridges following the form of the wax; thick creamy strokes on the drips, soft blended glazes where the wax turns translucent and light passes through it (warm subsurface glow like a lit candle). The flame is painted in confident thick gold and amber strokes with a soft halo bloomed into the wax below it. Edges are soft and hand-painted but the outer silhouette stays crisp and definite. Slightly rough, tactile, handmade — like a small still-life painted with love.

FACE: set low on the front of the body. Two large round dark eyes, glossy black-brown with a single warm highlight each, wide-set and honest, looking gently straight at you. No eyebrows. A tiny simple closed smile, calm and patient. Warm rosy-apricot blush smudged softly on both cheeks, as if warmed from the inside. The expression is steady, kind, a little sleepy — the face of something that has been quietly keeping watch for a long time and does not mind.

THE ONE IDEA: it is a candle burned almost all the way down, standing in the wide pool of everything it has already given — and it is still perfectly, cheerfully alight. Shorter than it started, softer at the edges, and utterly unbothered. The flame never wavers.

Full body, standing, symmetrical, gentle warm ambient glow, no props, no accessories, no text.
